description: A small stand of trees encountered as one place is a 'koshira'. It may be planted or self-sown, sacred or thoroughly ordinary. 'shelira' (forest) is the broader wooded landscape; 'lureko shiro koshira' specifies an orchard.
articulatory_notes: The back of the tongue seals briefly for 'k' and releases into rounded 'o'. The blade then rises for stressed unvoiced 'shi'; a tap or trill at the alveolar ridge opens into final 'a'.

semantic_domains.nature: a small stand of trees considered as one place
semantic_domains.spatial: a bounded or recognisable cluster of trees
